human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for grocery-specific customer-service boundary discipline, order/POS/payment mismatch handling, promotion and pricing evidence, food-safety and recall escalation, fraud and loss-prevention routing, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and retail-consumer-and-hospitality research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for service-support-and-resolution plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to grocery customer-service resolution, communication, and escalation packaging without pricing override, refund exception approval, food-safety signoff, SNAP/WIC interpretation, fraud adjudication, or loss-prevention action.

Authority sources cover retail food safety, recalls, online order delay and refund handling, deceptive pricing, price verification, SNAP EBT tender records, billing-error boundaries, identity-theft controls, and call-center payment-security handling with refresh and decay rules.

Scenario set covers missing evidence, order and payment mismatches, policy exceptions, repeat failures, pricing conflicts, food-safety cases, fraud-sensitive cases, high-volume intake, adjacent-lane handoff, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdictional, tenant, store, processor, supplier, local-health, recall, and SNAP/WIC differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
